ip_outcome_note: Card name is 'Broly, Dawn of the Rampage'; Era: Broly Saga; Special Trait: Saiyan. The depicted figure has massive musculature, long dark hair, torn dark clothing, and a green energy aura — all canonical visual identifiers of Broly in his pre-transformation state as depicted in Dragon Ball Z Movie 8 (1993). The card title 'Dawn of the Rampage' directly names the pre-powered moment before the Legendary Super Saiyan form erupts, consistent with the figure's subdued pre-transformation appearance. [Wikipedia: Dragon Ball Z: Broly – The Legendary Super Saiyan; WebSearch: TCGplayer BT1-076]

trivia_bullets.text: Galactic Battle (DBS-B01, July 2017) features two distinct arc pools: the Universe 6 / Champa Arc and the Broly Saga. The Broly Saga cards reference the 1993 non-canon film Dragon Ball Z: Broly – The Legendary Super Saiyan (Movie 8). In that film, Broly is revealed to have been born with a power level of 10,000 — extraordinarily high for a newborn — which prompted King Vegeta to order his execution. Paragus and Broly survived, ultimately seeking revenge on King Vegeta's bloodline. [Wikipedia: Dragon Ball Z: Broly – The Legendary Super Saiyan]
sources: Wikipedia: Dragon Ball Z: Broly – The Legendary Super Saiyan

trivia_bullets.text: BT1-076 is a green Common Battle card (1 energy, 5000 power). Its [Auto] on-play skill lets you look at up to 7 cards from the top of your deck and add 1 green Broly card to hand — functioning as a tutor specifically for the Broly archetype, enabling access to BT1-075 (Rampaging Super Saiyan Broly, 30000 power / Triple Strike) or the BT1-073 leader. [WebSearch: dbs-cardgame.com Galactic Battle Deck Breakdown Broly; WebSearch: DBZExchange BT1-076]
sources: WebSearch: dbs-cardgame.com Galactic Battle Deck Breakdown Broly

trivia_bullets.text: The original Broly (Movie 8) is described by Wikipedia as 'a non-canon major villain created by screenwriter Takao Koyama.' A canonical reimagining was introduced in the 2018 theatrical film Dragon Ball Super: Broly, which retconned the character into main Dragon Ball continuity. BT1-076 predates that film and references the classic non-canon Broly — a distinction the DBS TCG later reflected by printing both versions across sets. [Wikipedia: Broly; WebSearch: dbs-cardgame.com Galactic Battle Checklist]
